danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite opponents in flight arena
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion and combat response
Hazard that results in mission failure or damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Smooth sprite animation and control responsiveness
safety_signals.meaning: Safe operational state; player has tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Defined flight arena edges and non-hostile zones
safety_signals.meaning: Designated area where player can operate without environmental threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct enemy aircraft sprites with different designs per round
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military airfield environments; sky-based combat zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual pilot character portraits; unique aircraft designs per opponent
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stalin Fortimesky (Round 6 1P VS mode) with regionally-differentiated portrait shading
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy aircraft behavior patterns; opponent-specific dogfight arenas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage select functionality (accessible via Game Genie codes); sequential mission briefings with opponent portraits
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player faces progressively challenging enemy pilots across multiple rounds; 1P VS mode tracks opponent progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Invulnerability mode togglable (Game Genie code XTKLLIAV/XTOUYIAV/XTEUZIAV + A button hold)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player aircraft becomes immune to collision and enemy attacks; mission can be completed without danger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Congratulations screen triggers at 50,000+ points; Game Over screen at lower scores (European version conditional display)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scoring threshold determines narrative closure; regional variant affects end-state visual communication

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dogfight mode arena with visible opponent portrait and aircraft; 1P vs CPU or 2P vs player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One-on-one combat format replaces campaign structure; opponent AI or human player behavior determines engagement outcome

environmental_storytelling.example: Each round (especially 1P VS mode) displays individual enemy pilot character portraits (Stalin Fortimesky for Round 6) with regional shading variations, establishing narrative progression through recognizable antagonists
Distinct enemy aircraft sprites per opponent round signal increasing difficulty and regional/national military identities; visual variety prevents monotonous encounter structure
Flight arenas with implicit military airfield or combat zone settings; background design communicates geopolitical context without explicit dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to directional input; capable of rolling and maneuvering within bounded arena; sprite animation reflects flight dynamics
Ignores collision damage; immune to enemy projectiles; mission can proceed to guaranteed completion
Pursues and attacks player; AI follows combat patterns specific to opponent round; visual sprite movement indicates tactical positioning
Static display in pre-mission briefing; regional shading and character naming (Stalin Fortimesky) establish individual identity and geopolitical narrative

notes: Source material focuses on technical/development data rather than explicit visual communication mechanics. Extraction inferred from gameplay paradigm (action flight combat) and described features (roll mechanics, dogfight mode, opponent progression). Regional differences document minor graphical adjustments (pilot portrait shading) suggesting localization prioritized character identity. World-state communication relies on sprite animation, arena boundaries, score thresholds, and opponent gallery structure rather than UI-heavy state indicators typical of later action games. Game Genie codes reveal debug access to invulnerability and stage select, indicating developer-intentional manifestation layer for testing purposes.
